scope: Pass B review of the MailerSend developer documentation. This bundle covers the legacy email source streams plus documented Email API/account list and analytics endpoints that fit the declarative JSON read dialect. ID-scoped details, transactional send/verification/SMS operations, exports, and account/configuration mutations are excluded with closed categories.

excluded.reason: Detail lookups return the same object families as covered list streams and require caller-supplied IDs or emails instead of producing independent collections.
Domain subresources require per-domain fan-out and separate nested schemas outside the legacy domain/message/recipient record contract.
Account administration and monitoring endpoints require elevated account scopes beyond the default Email API source-read contract.
Email verification and SMS resources use separate product surfaces/scopes and are outside the legacy MailerSend email source connector contract.
Transactional email/SMS send endpoints deliver messages to recipients and require explicit send approval semantics, not a generic reverse-ETL write.
Bulk-email status is an async job-status lookup for a send request, not a syncable business-object collection.
Template, identity, inbound-route, user/invite, token, webhook, and verification actions mutate account configuration or initiate verification workflows.

endpoints.path: /templates/{template_id}, /identities/{identity_id}, /identities/email/{email}, /inbound/{inbound_id}, /users/{user_id}, /token/{token_id}, /webhooks/{webhook_id}, domain settings and SMS configuration update endpoints

excluded.reason: Update endpoints mutate sending templates, identities, routes, users, tokens, webhooks, domain settings, or SMS configuration.
Delete/cancel endpoints remove account configuration, users/invites/tokens, sender identities, routes, webhooks, scheduled sends, or product data.
